# Database Schema

This document describes the Firestore schema for the Symmetry News App backend.

---

## Collection: `articles`

Stores articles uploaded by journalists. Each document represents a single article.

**Document ID:** Auto-generated by Firestore (`string`)

### Fields

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `title` | `string` | âś… | Article headline |
| `author` | `string` | âś… | Display name of the journalist who wrote the article |
| `authorId` | `string` | âś… | Firebase Auth UID of the author; enforced by Firestore security rules |
| `authorPhotoURL` | `string` | âťŚ | Profile photo URL of the author (nullable) |
| `description` | `string` | âś… | Short summary of the article (shown in the list view) |
| `content` | `string` | âś… | Full body text of the article |
| `thumbnailURL` | `string` | âś… | Public download URL of the article image, stored in Firebase Cloud Storage under `media/articles/` |
| `publishedAt` | `timestamp` | âś… | Date the article was published (set by the journalist) |
| `createdAt` | `timestamp` | âś… | Firestore server timestamp set automatically when the document is created |
| `updatedAt` | `timestamp` | âś… | Firestore server timestamp set at creation (reserved for future update tracking) |
| `likeCount` | `number` | âś… | Denormalized count of likes; default `0`, updated atomically via Firestore transactions |
| `commentCount` | `number` | âś… | Denormalized count of comments; default `0`, updated atomically via Firestore transactions |

### Example Document

```json
{
  "title": "Breaking: Flutter 4.0 Released",
  "author": "Mario GarcĂ­a",
  "authorId": "uid-abc123",
  "authorPhotoURL": "https://firebasestorage.googleapis.com/v0/b/<project>.appspot.com/o/avatars%2Fuid-abc123.jpg?alt=media",
  "description": "The Flutter team announced a major release packed with performance improvements and new widgets.",
  "content": "Today, the Flutter team at Google announced the release of Flutter 4.0...",
  "thumbnailURL": "https://firebasestorage.googleapis.com/v0/b/<project>.appspot.com/o/media%2Farticles%2Fflutter-4.jpg?alt=media",
  "publishedAt": "2026-03-14T10:00:00Z",
  "createdAt": "2026-03-14T09:55:00Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-03-14T09:55:00Z",
  "likeCount": 12,
  "commentCount": 3
}
```

---

## Subcollection: `articles/{articleId}/likes`

Tracks which users have liked a given article. One document per user.

**Document ID:** Firebase Auth UID of the user who liked the article.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `likedAt` | `timestamp` | âś… | Server timestamp of when the like was created |

**Access pattern:** Read the document `likes/{uid}` to check if the current user has liked an article. A transaction atomically creates/deletes the like document and increments/decrements `likeCount` on the parent article.

---

## Subcollection: `articles/{articleId}/comments`

Stores comments posted on a journalist article.

**Document ID:** Auto-generated by Firestore.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `authorId` | `string` | âś… | Firebase Auth UID of the commenter |
| `authorName` | `string` | âś… | Display name of the commenter at the time of posting |
| `text` | `string` | âś… | Comment body (must be non-empty; enforced by Firestore rules) |
| `createdAt` | `timestamp` | âś… | Server timestamp of when the comment was created |

**Access pattern:** Comments are streamed in ascending `createdAt` order. Adding a comment atomically creates the comment document and increments `commentCount` on the parent article.

---

## Collection: `users/{uid}/bookmarks`

User-scoped bookmark references. Each document represents one bookmarked article for a specific user.

**Path:** `users/{uid}/bookmarks/{articleId}`
**Document ID:** The Firestore ID of the bookmarked article (or a client-generated key for API articles).

> This collection is managed entirely client-side via the bookmark service. Only the authenticated user matching `{uid}` can read or write their own bookmarks (enforced by Firestore security rules).

---

## Firebase Cloud Storage Structure

Article thumbnails are stored separately from the database to keep Firestore documents lightweight. Only the public download URL is stored in Firestore.

```
Firebase Cloud Storage
â””â”€â”€ media/
    â””â”€â”€ articles/
        â”śâ”€â”€ {timestamp}_{filename}.jpg
        â”śâ”€â”€ {timestamp}_{filename}.png
        â””â”€â”€ ...
```

**Constraints enforced by Storage rules:**
- Maximum file size: 5 MB
- Allowed content types: `image/*` (JPG, PNG, WebP, etc.)

---

## Design Decisions

### Why `thumbnailURL` instead of a Storage path reference?
Storing the full public download URL directly in Firestore makes it simpler to consume from the Flutter frontend â€” no extra SDK call is needed to resolve the URL before displaying the image.

### Why are `createdAt` and `updatedAt` separate from `publishedAt`?
`publishedAt` is the journalist's editorial decision of when the article should appear as published. `createdAt` and `updatedAt` are technical metadata managed by Firestore, useful for ordering, auditing, and future draft functionality.

### Why are `likeCount` and `commentCount` denormalized on the article?
Counting subcollection documents on every read would require an extra Firestore query per article. Denormalizing the counts into the parent document allows the feed to display counts in a single read. Firestore transactions guarantee the counts stay consistent when likes/comments are added or removed.

### Compatibility with the existing frontend

The Firestore fields map directly to the `ArticleEntity` used by the Flutter frontend (`lib/features/daily_news/domain/entities/article.dart`):

| Firestore field | `ArticleEntity` field |
|----------------|----------------------|
| `title` | `title` |
| `author` | `author` |
| `authorId` | `authorId` |
| `authorPhotoURL` | `authorPhotoURL` |
| `description` | `description` |
| `content` | `content` |
| `thumbnailURL` | `urlToImage` |
| `publishedAt` | `publishedAt` |
| `likeCount` | `likeCount` |
| `commentCount` | `commentCount` |
| *(doc ID)* | `firestoreId` |
